notes: Established. No tooling. Best for <500 units/month single SKU. Pure speed and cost advantage at low-moderate volumes.

market_premium_potential: 40–80% price premium vs base FDM product
notes: Post-FDM process — does not compete for floor space. Contract-shop validation ($100–150) recommended before purchase. LightBurn license $60 additional.
Smaller work area limits batch throughput. Better for personalization of individual pieces than batch production. 4-in-1 capability (inkjet/blade) adds non-laser versatility.
Currently sold out April 2026. Cloud-dependent architecture is a reliability risk for production. Highest capital cost in this tier. US-made/serviced. Not recommended at this stage.

technology: Resin printing — budget (Elegoo Saturn 4 Ultra 16K + wash/cure)
notes: Post-processing labor is primary cost driver (~20–25 min/part). Not viable for commodity cable clips — requires specialty product category with 2–3x price premium.
Closed ecosystem resin starting at $79/liter. Best reliability in class. Only justified if resin revenue >$800/month. Upgrade path from budget MSLA after validation.
From cnc-capabilities-analysis.md: not recommended before laser/resin validation. Material waste 30–70%. New QC failure modes (tool wear drift). Floor space competes directly with FDM expansion.

technology: Injection molding — outsourced (Fictiv single-cavity aluminum mold)
payback_period_months_at_300_mo: Mold payback 4.6–9.2 months depending on volume
notes: Break-even vs FDM: ~4600 cumulative units (single-cavity $3000 mold). Only justifiable at 500+ units/month. Lead time: 10–15 days T1 samples (Fictiv). Design must be frozen before tooling commitment.
